topics.why_needed: To explain the statutory authority, structure, annuity mechanics, and valuation framework for a Grantor Retained Annuity Trust (GRAT) in the client deliverable.

queries.q: IRC 2702 grantor retained annuity trust requirements
26 CFR 25.2702-3 GRAT annuity interest valuation qualified interest
IRC 2702 qualified interest definition fixed annuity amount requirements

exclusion_rules: Exclude practitioner blogs or law firm summaries
expected_citable_outputs: Valuation method for retained annuity interest

topics.why_needed: To define a Charitable Remainder Annuity Trust (CRAT), its fixed annuity structure, payout limits, and charitable remainder requirements.

queries.q: IRC 664 charitable remainder annuity trust definition annuity trust
26 CFR 1.664-2 charitable remainder annuity trust requirements
IRC 664 5 percent minimum payout 50 percent maximum present value annuity interest

expected_citable_outputs: 50% maximum present value rule for annuity interest
Requirement that remainder pass to qualified charity

topics.why_needed: To compare how GRATs and CRATs affect gift and estate tax exposure for a married client with $16M liquidity and 2015 exemption thresholds.

queries.q: IRC 2501 gift tax imposed general rule
IRC 2033 gross estate includes property interests of decedent
2015 federal estate tax exemption amount 40 percent rate IRS

expected_citable_outputs: 2015 estate tax exemption amounts and 40% rate confirmation

topics.why_needed: To explain how the Section 7520 rate is used to value retained annuity interests and charitable remainder interests.
expected_citable_outputs: Statutory authority for valuation using 7520 rate
Reference to mortality tables and valuation methodology

topics.why_needed: To explain income taxation of CRAT distributions (tier system) if supported by allowlisted sources.

queries.q: IRC 664 taxation of charitable remainder trusts distributions character of income
IRS charitable remainder trust tier system ordinary income capital gain tax-exempt income return of principal

exclusion_rules: Exclude academic commentary unless from IRS publications
expected_citable_outputs: Order of taxation of distributions (tier system)
Definition of income categories within CRT framework

topics.why_needed: To document statutory and structural risks relevant to GRATs and CRATs for a 62-year-old client.

queries.q: GRAT grantor dies during term estate inclusion risk
CRAT actuarial requirements fail qualification 10 percent remainder requirement
proposals to change IRC 2702 GRAT rules minimum term remainder requirement

expected_citable_outputs: Estate inclusion consequences if grantor dies during term
Actuarial compliance requirements for CRAT qualification

topics.why_needed: To ensure deliverable includes appropriate technical and tax-law limitation language consistent with IRS authority.

queries.q: Circular 230 31 CFR Subtitle A Part 10 written advice standards disclaimer
IRS reliance disclaimer written tax advice Circular 230 guidance

open_questions.question: Confirm authoritative IRS source listing 2015 federal estate tax exemption and 40% rate.
open_questions.impact_if_missing: Cannot numerically anchor estate tax exposure discussion.
open_questions.best_allowlisted_place_to_find: irs.gov historical estate tax exemption tables

open_questions.question: Confirm exact statutory language for 5% minimum and 50% maximum CRAT payout limitations.
open_questions.impact_if_missing: Cannot safely describe CRAT qualification rules.

open_questions.question: Identify explicit regulation referencing estate inclusion if grantor dies during GRAT term.
open_questions.impact_if_missing: Risk analysis may lack statutory support.
open_questions.best_allowlisted_place_to_find: law.cornell.edu CFR under Section 2702 or estate inclusion provisions

open_questions.question: Identify an allowlisted primary source (irs.gov/treasury.gov/law.cornell.edu) discussing enacted legislative changes or authoritative proposals affecting GRAT rules under IRC 2702, if any are relevant to include as legislative risk.
open_questions.impact_if_missing: Legislative risk discussion must be minimized or removed to avoid unsupported claims.
open_questions.best_allowlisted_place_to_find: treasury.gov or irs.gov official releases; law.cornell.edu for enacted statutory text
